---
title: "Weekly notes"
subtitle: "STAT 45203 · one method page per week"
toc: false
---

::: {.source-basis}
Original instructor-authored notes. Data is synthetic. Open texts are conceptual companions cited by
section only (see [Open readings & attribution](../resources/reading-list.qmd)). Ungraded; Blackboard is
authoritative for graded work.
:::

Each week's page follows the same shape: a **picture and a worked example first**, then the idea in
plain language with live-text math, a **common mistake** to avoid, ungraded practice, and a short bridge
to the next week. The through-line is a single question — *what is fragile here, and what can we still
say?*

## First half — seeing variability without a formula

1. [Why assumption-light methods?](week-01-why-assumption-light.qmd) — models, assumptions, and reading
   a dataset for what is fragile.
2. [Order statistics, ranks, and empirical distributions](week-02-order-statistics-ranks-ecdf.qmd) —
   quantiles, the median, ranks, and the ECDF as a distribution-free summary.
3. [Permutation logic](week-03-permutation-logic.qmd) — what a null hypothesis lets us shuffle, and what
   it must hold fixed.
4. [Randomization tests](week-04-randomization-tests.qmd) — design-based inference and *p*-values from
   simulated reassignments.
5. [Bootstrap distributions](week-05-bootstrap-distributions.qmd) — resampling with replacement to see
   how a statistic wobbles.
6. [Bootstrap confidence intervals](week-06-bootstrap-confidence-intervals.qmd) — percentile intervals,
   what they assume, and where they fail.
7. [Rank-based one-sample and paired methods](week-07-rank-based-one-sample-paired.qmd) — sign and
   signed-rank tests for one sample and matched pairs. *(Midterm this week.)*

## Second half — ranks, robustness, and comparison

8. [Two-sample rank methods](week-08-two-sample-rank-methods.qmd) — the rank-sum test and what a
   location shift does and does not claim.
9. [Categorical and ordinal outcomes](week-09-categorical-ordinal-outcomes.qmd) — contingency tables,
   exact logic, and honoring the measurement scale.
10. [Robust summaries and outliers](week-10-robust-summaries-outliers.qmd) — median, trimmed mean, MAD,
    and reading influence without deleting data.
11. [Robust regression ideas](week-11-robust-regression-ideas.qmd) — why least squares is sensitive, and
    what a resistant fit changes.
12. [Comparing parametric and nonparametric conclusions](week-12-comparing-parametric-nonparametric.qmd)
    — agreement, divergence, and honest reporting.
13. [Simulation study of method behavior](week-13-simulation-study-method-behavior.qmd) — Type I error,
    power, bias, and coverage as things you can *measure*.
14. [Applied robust-methods report](week-14-applied-robust-methods-report.qmd) — the project workshop:
    turning a comparison into a defensible report.
15. [Final review](week-15-final-review.qmd) — synthesis: choosing a method for a purpose.
